name: Follow-Up Nudge Templates
description: Templates for gentle follow-up messages when a patient pauses or stops responding during a conversation.

first_nudge_variants.text: Just checking in -- we're still here if you need anything. No rush at all. Just reply anytime!
Hi {FirstName}! Just wanted to make sure you got our message. Happy to help if you have any questions. No pressure!
Hi! We're still here if you'd like help scheduling or have any questions. Just reply whenever you're ready.

second_nudge_variants.text: Hi {FirstName}! Just one more check-in from {ClinicName}. We're here whenever you're ready -- no expiration on that. Have a great day!
Hey! Just a quick note from {ClinicName} -- whenever you're ready to chat, we're just a text away. No rush!

mid_conversation_pause_variants.description: For when a patient pauses mid-conversation (e.g., during booking flow).

variants.text: Still here if you'd like to continue! No rush at all.
Take your time! I'll be here whenever you're ready to pick back up.
Just wanted to let you know I'm still here. If you need to come back to this later, just reply anytime.

after_insurance_question_variants.description: For when a patient asked about insurance and then went quiet.

variants.text: Hi {FirstName}! Just following up -- would you like us to go ahead and check on your insurance benefits? It's quick and no obligation.
Hey! If you're still thinking about the insurance question, we're happy to verify your benefits whenever you're ready. Just say the word!

never: Create urgency or imply they're missing out
Make the patient feel guilty for not responding
Use language like 'we haven't heard from you'
always: Make it clear there's no pressure
Leave the door open without demanding a response
